mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with t-stat > 2.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where unemployment rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets price in improved economic output.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 5-day returns in > 60% of observations during tightening cycles, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 310 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 310 consistently coincides with positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market absorption, driving consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in > 65% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes yield positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.
